ogTitle: Centenary Heights vs Chahpingah: Climate Safety

heading: Centenary Heights vs Chahpingah — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Centenary Heights, Queensland and Chahpingah, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Centenary Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Chahpingah (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Centenary Heights or Chahpingah safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Centenary Heights has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Chahpingah scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Centenary Heights scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Chahpingah scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Centenary Heights or Chahpingah?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Centenary Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
